title: Modern Platform Bed with Chunky Legs
summary: A queen platform bed built from a 1x8 rail box with mitered 2x6 corner supports and stubby 2x6 leg blocks — a simpler, boxier build than this catalog's other platform beds.
description:
This platform bed keeps things simple: a 1x8 box for the rails, 45°-cut 2x6 corner supports that double as the bed's structural corners and its leg-mounting blocks, and a center support to keep a queen-size deck from sagging. Build the box first and check it for square before the corner supports go in — those supports are what the legs attach to later, so the box's squareness at this stage determines whether the finished bed sits level.

The cleats that carry the slats mount 2" down from the top of the side rails, on the inside face, screwed every 6 to 8 inches — consistent spacing here is what keeps the slat deck from developing a wave under weight. The legs are genuinely simple stub blocks that screw straight into the corner supports from inside the box; the plan notes you could swap in a different foot style using the same corner-support attachment method, which makes this a reasonable base for style variations.

Slats go on last, spaced about 4" apart, screwed both to the cleats and to the center support — that center attachment point is what keeps the middle of a queen-size deck from flexing independently of the sides.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Build the rectangular box from the end and side rails.

steps.title: Add the corner supports
steps.body: Adjust the bed frame for square and fasten the corner supports into the corners, keeping top edges flush — this becomes the bottom of the bed frame.

steps.title: Add the center support
steps.body: Mark the end rails 30" in from each side and fasten the center support between them with 2" screws and glue, keeping top edges flush.

steps.title: Attach the cleats
steps.body: Flip the bed frame over and mark the side rails down 2" from the top on both sides. Attach the cleats with 2" screws and glue, screwing every 6-8 inches.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Line up the leg bases with the outside corners of the bed frame and screw through the corner supports into the top of the leg blocks, using at least 2 screws per leg. Add the leg trim around each leg base.

steps.title: Add the slats and finish
steps.body: Lay the slats on top of the cleats, spaced about 4" apart, and screw down — also screwing into the center support. Fill any holes, sand, and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Platform bed with a 1x8 rail box and chunky stub legs
